pattern: Precomputing the best left and best right window starting indices for every middle window position turns a triple search into a single linear sweep that picks the lexicographically smallest optimal triple.
hints: Fix the middle window first; then you only need the best non-overlapping window on each side.
Use a sliding window sum so every length-k block sum is available in linear time.
Scan from the left tracking the earliest index giving the max prefix window, scan from the right for the suffix window, then try each middle start and combine, preferring smaller indices on ties.

edge_inputs.description: all equal values forces leftmost choice
tie between front and back windows

stress.description: random values with valid k = length // 3
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(2000, size))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    k = max(1, n // 3)
    return [nums, k]
